_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — Royal-Grade bridge from Knight Champion (L9) to L9.5 Royal-Grade. Apply 3 ADDITIVE adds — gold-spiked fin caps, gold cross emblem on cuirass medial ridge, and fins grow taller and more sharply curved (volume bump replaces deprecated knuckle-plate + gilt-edge-trim passes per AD detail-economy).
_meta.input_image: image_urls[0] = UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L9/composite/cavalry_L9_v1_composited.png ; image_urls[1] = UnitProgression/Cavalry/Refs/L1_Base.png (scale anchor) — placeholder keeper version, user will rewire to actual keeper at runtime.
history: v1 (2026-05-07): initial author — L9.5 Royal-Grade Bridge with 3 ADDITIVE adds per CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § K0 + AD FeedbackV0 detail-economy. (1) Gold-spiked fin caps (~1.5 inch gold cones at the apex of each L9 fin). (2) Gold cross emblem on cuirass chest (four-armed cross centered on the L7 cuirass medial ridge, painted in warm gold inlay against the steel face). (3) Fins grow taller (~7-8 inches) and more sharply curved (volume bump replaces the deprecated 'gold gauntlet knuckle-plates' and 'gilt edge trim on cuirass + tassets + chamfron' passes — both REMOVED per AD detail-economy). Gold accents at L9.5 = helm gold band (L8) + fin gold spike tips (new) + pennon gold trim + lance socket gold ring (L9) + cuirass gold cross (new). Cleaner gold map; volumes carry the upgrade.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy.
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.

v7 (2026-05-07): scrub stale "3-lame layered pauldrons" PRESERVE references — L4-L10 pauldron progression now reads as single continuous piece (L4 disc → L6 thicker dome → L8 dome + bicep extension → L10 dome+extension grown in volume). No stacked-plate construction language anywhere in the chain.

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.
v9 (2026-05-07): residual chest-band PRESERVE/preamble references scrubbed.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).

v7 (2026-05-07): replace L9.5 GOLD-SPIKED FIN CAPS positive add — model consistently misread the spike geometry on the helm fins. Replaced with GOLD-TRIM BAND ALONG THE LOWER EDGE OF THE CAPARISON (single ~1" buttery gold strip on the existing red+blue checkered caparison's tabbed lower hem). A small royal-tier preview beat heralding the next-tier full caparison recolor. Fins remain plain steel; gold spike caps removed and explicit negative added.

adds.title: GOLD-TRIM BAND ALONG THE LOWER EDGE OF THE CAPARISON
adds.summary: add a single thin buttery polished yellow gold metal strip (~1" wide) running along the entire lower tabbed hem of the existing red+blue checkered caparison. A small royal-tier preview beat heralding the full recolored heraldic caparison at the next tier.

adds.title: GOLD CROSS EMBLEM ON CUIRASS CHEST
adds.summary: add a four-armed gold CROSS centered on the L7 cuirass's medial ridge, sitting at the center of the chest at sternum height.

adds.title: FINS GROW TALLER AND MORE SHARPLY CURVED
adds.summary: extend the L9 ~5-inch backswept fins UPWARD to ~7-8 inches tall, and increase their backward+outward curvature so each fin reads more sharply hooked at the trailing tip.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L9/composite/cavalry_L9_v1_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply THREE visible ADDITIVE Royal-Grade upgrades to the mounted cavalry knight in the FIRST input image, bridging from Knight Champion to the existing Royal-Grade. Every the levels above detail must remain visible at this level. The rider already wears the existing Knight Champion kit: kettle helm with backswept neck-guard skirt + brim reinforcement + additional brim rivets + buttery gold trim band at the brim line + half-visor + closed visor extending downward (face fully concealed) + backswept fins (~5 inches tall) on the kettle's temples; steel bevor at the throat; full steel cuirass with medial ridge and 1 horizontal abdominal seam; skirt band of yellow+red livery only (narrowed) — cuirass sits flush against the bevor (NO chest band of cloth above it); full-arm scale-mail sleeves bicep-to-wrist; single-piece steel pauldrons on both shoulders (thicker domes + bicep coverage extension, no stacked-plate construction); steel vambraces on both forearms; steel finger-plates + clean knuckle disc layered on top of bare-hand silhouettes (with leather grip cord still visible between gauntleted fingers and wooden haft); leather waist belt with plain leather belt and brass buckle; longer 2-wider-lame steel tassets at both hips covering mid-thigh; full steel sabatons on both boots; blue trousers; brown leather riding boots in stirrups; wooden spear haft (warm-brown wood, with all accumulated kit + steel leaf-tip head + red+yellow heraldic pennon with gold trim + gold tassel + gold ring at lance socket); hip pouch on spear-side hip (the only belt accessory). The horse already wears: full steel chamfron with forehead spike, steel crinet on the back of the neck, plain-steel flanchards on both flanks, iron-buckled bridle with iron browband, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle, iron peytral plate beneath the red+blue checkered caparison (central panel still visible between flanchards). THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — no white markings of any kind. CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side. Do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement. (1) GOLD-TRIM BAND ALONG THE LOWER EDGE OF THE CAPARISON — add a single thin BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL strip running along the entire lower hem of the cloth caparison covering the horse's body. The gold band is approximately 1 inch wide, hugging the bottom edge of the caparison's tabbed lower edge (the zigzag/scalloped lower edge of the cloth). RENDER THE GOLD AS BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow contrasting strongly against the red+blue checkered cloth — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base, (ii) soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow, (iii) deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. The caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, steel-blue trim band, tabbed lower edge shape, and overall drape ALL remain unchanged — the only addition is the single thin gold strip along the very bottom edge. This is a royal-tier preview beat: a small gold accent on the horse heralding the full recolored heraldic caparison that arrives at the next-tier upgrade. (2) GOLD CROSS EMBLEM ON CUIRASS CHEST — add a four-armed gold CROSS centered on the existing cuirass's medial ridge, sitting at the center of the chest at sternum height. The cross has four equal arms each ~2.5 inches long and ~0.8 inch wide, with simple straight ends (no fleur-de-lis decoration, no ornate flares — clean four-armed equilateral cross). The cross is painted in warm gold INLAY against the steel face of the cuirass — the gold is flush with the steel surface (NOT a raised emblem; an inlay reading as a flat gold shape on the steel). The cross sits atop the existing medial ridge so the vertical arm of the cross aligns with the ridge's axis and the horizontal arm crosses the ridge perpendicularly. RENDER THE GOLD as buttery polished yellow gold per Edit Goal item (1). (3) FINS GROW TALLER AND MORE SHARPLY CURVED — extend the ~5-inch backswept fins UPWARD to ~7-8 inches tall, and increase their backward+outward curvature so each fin reads more sharply hooked at the trailing tip. Each fin's leading edge still attaches at the kettle's temple per; the new taller height and sharper curve add VOLUME to the upper helm silhouette (silhouette mightiness — preparing for the existing plumed crest). The new gold-spike caps at each fin's apex (Edit Goal item 1) sit at the new TALLER apex, not the ~5-inch apex. The fins remain neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) except for the new gold spike caps. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: NOT VISIBLE — fully concealed by the existing closed visor (preserved at this level). NO skin, NO hair, NO eyes, NO mustache visible above the bevor. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the the levels above kettle helm with all accumulated additions (backswept neck-guard skirt + brim reinforcement + additional brim rivets, half-visor at the upper face, buttery gold trim band at the brim line, closed visor extending downward to fully conceal the face, backswept fins on the temples — at the fins are GROWN TALLER per Edit Goal item 3 and gain gold spike caps per Edit Goal item 1). THE RIDER NECK: the existing steel bevor / collar plate at the throat, unchanged. THE RIDER TORSO: the existing reinforced cuirass with medial ridge + 1 horizontal abdominal seam (the new gold cross emblem is added centered on the medial ridge per Edit Goal item 2); the skirt band of yellow+red livery (still visible as a narrow band) — cuirass sits flush against the bevor (NO chest band of cloth above it); the wide brown leather waist belt with brass buckle (plain leather, no studs). THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the EXISTING FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves; the EXISTING single-piece steel pauldrons on both shoulders (single continuous piece of shaped steel, + +, unchanged — NO gold rivets, NO gold seam piping); the EXISTING steel vambraces on the forearms; the EXISTING steel finger-plates + CLEAN knuckle disc layered on top of bare-hand silhouettes; the existing leather grip cord still visible between gauntleted fingers and the wooden haft; both hands gripping the wooden spear haft and the brown leather reins. THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue trousers visible at the calf below the existing full sabatons; chunky warm-brown leather riding boots — body still visible at calf and heel above sabaton; the EXISTING full steel sabatons on both boots; both boots in stirrups; EXISTING longer 2-wider-lame steel tassets at both hips covering mid-thigh. THE WEAPON: the wooden spear haft warm-brown wood (the levels above unchanged) with the existing iron pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings + reinforced socket, steel leaf-tip head, red+yellow heraldic pennon with gold trim + gold tassel + gold ring at lance socket — all preserved unchanged at this level. (No new gold on the lance at this level — the existing full gilt finish on the lance head arrives later.) THE REINS: the brown leather reins at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed by the gauntleted rein-side hand. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT — head and muzzle on the rein-side, rump and tail on the spear-side, four-square stance, neck arched, ears forward. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling. Mane visible as a thin line between the existing crinet's lames; tail dark brown extending behind the rump. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with iron banding on pommel and cantle; the iron-buckled bridle with iron browband; the existing full steel chamfron with forehead spike — UNCHANGED at this level (NO gilt edge trim on the chamfron — REMOVED; gold-rosette boss arrives at this level); the existing steel crinet on the neck (UNCHANGED — NO per-lame gold piping); the existing plain-steel flanchards on both flanks (UNCHANGED — NO heraldic stamps, NO gold edge trim, NO emblem at this level); the existing iron peytral plate beneath the caparison. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison with red+blue checkered pattern and steel-blue trim band, central panel still visible between the flanchards on the rump — UNCHANGED at this level (the caparison color recolor to red+yellow heraldic with central gold cross arrives at this level). THE EXISTING the levels above KIT: ALL preserved unchanged in shape, color, and position. NO knife sheath at any hip (removed at). The hip pouch remains as the only belt accessory. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately 15-25 degrees toward the viewer's left. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the gauntleted hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing leather grip cord wrap. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down; the gauntleted hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance, with a clearly mightier silhouette than — taller and sharper-curved fins with gold spike caps, gold cross emblem on chest. NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly — with vertical extent acceptable from the existing taller fins) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 78-80% of canvas vertical height (small upward growth from the taller fins). The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the steel leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT crop the helm fins or hooves. Clean opaque WHITE background — no light gray tint, no off-white shading. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT add gold gauntlet knuckle-plates, raised gold knuckle-discs, per-knuckle gold caps, or any gold decoration on the gauntlets at this level — the gauntlet knuckle disc remains CLEAN steel (this beat was REMOVED — surface ornament with low thumbnail read at gauntlet scale); DO NOT add gilt edge trim, gold edge piping, or gold perimeter line on the cuirass perimeter, tasset perimeters, chamfron perimeter, vambrace cuffs, sabaton edges, crinet lame edges, or any other plate edge at this level — per-edge gold piping should not appear; DO NOT add gold rivets to pauldrons or any other plate seam; DO NOT add gold filigree, scrollwork, chased patterns, engraved motifs, or sculpted figural emboss to any surface at this level; DO NOT add a tall plumed crest at this level (that arrives at this level); DO NOT add a 4th uppermost pauldron lame at this level (that arrives at this level); DO NOT add lion's-head or any other figural emboss to the pauldrons; DO NOT recolor the caparison at this level (it remains red+blue checkered; the recolor to red+yellow heraldic arrives at this level); DO NOT add the gold-rosette chamfron boss at this level (that arrives at this level); DO NOT stamp or emboss a heraldic shape on the flanchards (they remain plain steel); DO NOT add gold-trim per-lame piping at every crinet lame edge — the crinet stays plain steel; DO NOT make the cuirass gold cross taller than ~2.5 inches per arm or any shape other than a clean four-armed equilateral cross (no fleur-de-lis flares, no ornate cross variants); DO NOT make the cross raised / 3D / embossed — it is FLAT GOLD INLAY against the steel; DO NOT extend the cuirass gold cross beyond the medial ridge area to cover larger portions of the cuirass; DO NOT replace the kettle helm — the kettle stays at with all accumulated additions; DO NOT show the rider's face at this level — fully concealed by the existing closed visor; DO NOT make the fins shorter than the ~5-inch baseline (they GROW taller at this level); DO NOT replace the wooden spear haft with a steel haft — the wooden haft STAYS warm-brown wood; DO NOT add a full gilt finish on the lance head at this level (that arrives at this level); DO NOT add gold haft end-caps on the wooden spear at this level (those arrive at this level); DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, dagger, or any second belt accessory; DO NOT change the horse coat color from solid chestnut brown; DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT remove the existing crinet, flanchards, chamfron, or peytral; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, steel-blue trim band, or tabbed lower edge at this level (recolor arrives at this level); DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the camera angle or composition; DO NOT crop the helm fins or hooves; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, or add a ground plane; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, light-gray, off-white, or any non-white color; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
